perturbation.original_text: "Efficiency" means the quotient of E/F, where E = the electricity produced by the Portfolio, measured in BTUs (British Thermal Units) at a conversion rate of 3,412 BTUs per kWh, and F = the fuel consumed by the Portfolio, measured in BTUs on a Lower Heating Value basis.
perturbation.changed_text: "Efficiency" means the quotient of E/F, where E = the electricity produced by the Portfolio, measured in BTUs (British Thermal Units) at a conversion rate of 3,412 BTUs per kWh, and F = the fuel consumed by the Portfolio, measured in BTUs on a Higher Heating Value basis.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition uses Lower Heating Value (LHV) for fuel consumption. The modification changes this to Higher Heating Value (HHV). This creates a contradiction because efficiency calculations will now differ depending on which definition is used. This change directly impacts Section 2.7 (Efficiency Warranty) because the efficiency warranty calculation is now ambiguous, and also Section 2.8, where the Gas Payment Shortfall is determined.
perturbation.contradicted_text: During the Warranty Period, Operator shall determine for each full calendar month (the "Efficiency Warranty Period") within five (5) Business Days after the end of such month whether the Portfolio has performed at the Minimum Efficiency Level (the "Efficiency Warranty"); provided that the Efficiency Bank shall be utilized to the extent necessary to meet the Efficiency Warranty. If the Minimum Efficiency Level has not been met during such Efficiency Warranty Period, then Operator shall so notify Owner in writing of the basis of its determination and Owner may make a claim under Section 2.5. An example of an Efficiency Warranty calculation for purposes of a Section 2.5 claim is attached as Appendix E.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the definition of "Efficiency" from using Lower Heating Value (LHV) to Higher Heating Value (HHV) for fuel consumption. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which relies on a consistent and unambiguous definition of "Efficiency" for determining the Minimum Efficiency Level. The efficiency warranty calculation (as indicated by Appendix E) now becomes ambiguous, potentially leading to disputes regarding whether the Minimum Efficiency Level has been met. Since the efficiency warranty relies on a clear definition of efficiency, changing that definition creates a contradiction because the warranty calculation is now unclear. Therefore this change directly impacts Section 2.7 (Efficiency Warranty) because the efficiency warranty calculation is now ambiguous, and also Section 2.8, where the Gas Payment Shortfall is determined.

perturbation.original_text: Operator shall ensure that all operations and maintenance functions contemplated by this Section are performed by technically competent and qualified personnel (the "Service Technicians"). Operator shall ensure that all Service Technicians: (i) participate in a maintenance training program and receive confirmation of having achieved the requisite level of proficiency for the tasks they are assigned to perform, and (ii) attend periodic "refresher" training programs. The Operator shall at all times retain an operations manager who shall be dedicated to the overall supervision and management of performance of its obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Operator may, at its discretion, ensure that operations and maintenance functions are performed by personnel deemed appropriate (the "Service Technicians"). Operator is not obligated to provide any specific training programs for Service Technicians. The Operator shall endeavor to retain an operations manager who shall be dedicated to the overall supervision and management of performance of its obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates technically competent personnel and specific training. This modification introduces ambiguity. The word 'ensure' has been changed to 'may', making the requirement of qualified personal discretionary. The requirement of 'maintenance training programs' has been completely removed and the mandatory 'shall' retain an operation manager has been changed to 'shall endeavor to retain' which does not mandate a person to be retained to perform such service. This contradicts the intent of maintaining the facilities in good condition, as the level of competence and consistency in training is now uncertain. This ambiguity affects the interpretation of 'Prudent Electrical Practices' within Section 2.2 and the Performance Standards described in Section 2.16
perturbation.contradicted_text: Operator is hereby granted the right and authority (and, to the extent necessary to carry out its functions hereunder, a limited power of attorney) and agrees, for the benefit of Owner, to operate safely and reliably the Facilities and to maintain during the Warranty Period in accordance with the terms of this Agreement each Facility in good condition and repair in accordance with the Warranty Specifications and Prudent Electrical Practices. During the Warranty Period, the specific responsibilities of Operator under this Agreement shall include the following: (a) Facility Operations. Operator shall ensure that all Facility components are operated and maintained in a manner designed to meet the Efficiency Warranty and the Power Performance Warranty with a goal of achieving the performance levels assumed in the Base Case Model (as defined in the Company LLC Agreement). (b) Facility Maintenance. Operator shall perform, or cause to be performed, all scheduled and unscheduled maintenance required on each Facility in order to meet the Warranty Specifications. In that regard, Operator's responsibilities hereunder shall include, without limitation, promptly correcting any Bloom System or BOF malfunctions, either by (i) recalibrating or resetting the malfunctioning Bloom System or BOF, or (ii) repairing or replacing Bloom System or BOF components which are defective, damaged, worn or otherwise in need of replacement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens the requirements for personnel qualifications and training, and for retaining an operations manager. This contradicts the original requirement in the contradicted text that the Operator maintain the Facilities in good condition and repair, operated by qualified staff. The change from 'ensure' to 'may' and removing mandatory training creates ambiguity that directly conflicts with the requirement to maintain the facility in 'good condition and repair in accordance with Prudent Electrical Practices,' as qualified personnel are essential for this.

perturbation.original_text: Owner shall compensate Operator for the Facility Services for each Facility, on an annual basis at the rate specified in Exhibit A hereto (the "Service Fees"). With respect to each year of such Facility's Warranty Period, the Service Fees shall be invoiced not later than thirty (30) days prior to the end of the calendar month in which the anniversary of the date of Commencement of Operations for such Bloom System occurred, and shall be payable within thirty (30) days of invoice. Interest shall accrue daily on the Service Fees not paid when due, at the lesser of the monthly rate of one and five-tenths percent (1.5%) or the highest rate permissible by law on such unpaid balance. Operator shall be under no obligation to provide or perform services hereunder for any Bloom System whose Service Fee has not been paid in full for the then-current warranty year.
perturbation.changed_text: Owner shall compensate Operator for the Facility Services for each Facility, on an annual basis at the rate specified in Exhibit A hereto (the "Service Fees"), subject to adjustments based on Owner's satisfaction with service quality as reasonably determined by Owner. With respect to each year of such Facility's Warranty Period, the Service Fees shall be invoiced at a time agreed upon by both parties, and shall be payable within a timeframe mutually determined at the time of invoicing. Interest may accrue on the Service Fees not paid when due, at a rate to be negotiated if payment is delayed. Operator's obligation to provide services hereunder for any Bloom System may be suspended if Service Fees are significantly overdue, subject to good-faith negotiation.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies a clear compensation structure, invoicing timeline, payment terms and consequences of non-payment. The modified text introduces flexibility and subjectivity, removing the certainty of these terms. The phrase 'subject to adjustments based on Owner's satisfaction' introduces a subjective element. The specific timeline 'thirty days prior' has been changed to 'at a time agreed upon by both parties', reducing the obligation to the operator on when to invoice. 'shall be payable within thirty days' is no longer applicable and has been replaced with 'shall be payable within a timeframe mutually determined'. Lastly, it gives the opportunity for the Operator to provide services at their discretion 'may be suspended if service fees are significantly overdue', rather than the absolute in the original text. This creates ambiguity and potential disputes regarding payment and service obligations in Section 2.3(a) and calls into question the nature of the Service Fees.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Service Fees. (a) Owner shall compensate Operator for the Facility Services for each Facility, on an annual basis at the rate specified in Exhibit A hereto (the "Service Fees"). With respect to each year of such Facility's Warranty Period, the Service Fees shall be invoiced not later than thirty (30) days prior to the end of the calendar month in which the anniversary of the date of Commencement of Operations for such Bloom System occurred, and shall be payable within thirty (30) days of invoice. Interest shall accrue daily on the Service Fees not paid when due, at the lesser of the monthly rate of one and five-tenths percent (1.5%) or the highest rate permissible by law on such unpaid balance. Operator shall be under no obligation to provide or perform services hereunder for any Bloom System whose Service Fee has not been paid in full for the then-current warranty year. (b) In connection with Facility Services for the BOF, Operator shall provide all required labor but shall charge Owner for, and Owner shall reimburse Operator for, the cost of all required spare parts. Billing for such spare parts shall be done in the same manner as Services Fees, as set forth in Section 2.3(a).
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces significant ambiguities and subjective elements regarding payment terms and service obligations. The original text specifies a clear payment structure, invoicing timeline, and consequences of non-payment, as reiterated in the 'Contradicted Text'. The changed text replaces these fixed terms with phrases like 'subject to adjustments based on Owner's satisfaction', 'at a time agreed upon by both parties', and 'shall be payable within a timeframe mutually determined'. This directly contradicts the clear, pre-defined terms in the contradicted section (specifically the 'Service Fees' and billing timelines in Section 2.3(a)), creating uncertainty and potential for disputes. The move from 'shall' to 'may' regarding interest and service suspension further exacerbates the contradiction.
